preamble: The shift from the lateral approximant [l] to the labiodental fricative [v] is a specialized form of L-vocalization and consonant strengthening. It typically involves an intermediate stage where the 'dark L' [ɫ] vocalizes into a labio-velar glide [w], which then strengthens into a fricative.

examples.note: Interaction between vocalized liquids and labial fricatives in terminal clusters.
